free_text: Cross sections for (alpha,n) reactions for medium-
Variable energy a-particles obtained by acceleration
of doubly ionized He in ORNL 5.5-MV Van de Graaf.
The He++ beam was led through a 3-ft-thick water-wall
shield to reduce the neutron background.
Maximum beam current at the target was 0.25 mcA.
Matural and isotopically enriched targets prepared by
In all cases the target were sufficiently thick to
Efficiency variation with neutron energy was
believed to be within 1% for 1 keV to 2 MeV. At E(n)
= 5 MeV efficiency decreases by about 6% over that
Over-all efficiency of the detector approximately 3%,
Precise absolute value determined to 4% by calibration
The graphite sphere detector automatically averages
the angular distribution of neutrons emitted by the
It was estimated that an unidirectional beam of emitted
neutrons would be detected with an efficiency which is
only 1-2%  different from an isotropic distribution.
Error due to uncertainty in efficiency
Data taken from Table II of Phys.Rev.133,B911.
DR Name of the first author corrected
DR Reference corrected to follow APS format
OS. Corrected REACTION and units in
even-numbered subentries, added DERIV to odd-numbered
subentries, added ANALYSIS to odd-numbered subentries
